deep_dive.title: SovereignPA-Bench: Evaluating User-Owned Personal Agents under Evolving Intent, Platform Mediation, and Consent Constraints
deep_dive.abstract: Personal agents are becoming persistent user-owned intermediaries: they remember preferences, filter platform-mediated information, use tools, and negotiate with services. Existing benchmarks evaluate tool use, web navigation, desktop control, personalization, recommendation, and evolving context, but rarely ask whether an agent preserves user sovereignty: advancing the user's current interests while respecting privacy, consent, evidence, user burden, and resistance to manipulative incentives. We introduce SovereignPA-Bench, an executable benchmark for evaluating user-owned personal agents under evolving intent, platform mediation, privacy boundaries, consent constraints, evidence requirements, and burden tradeoffs. The benchmark separates agent-visible ObservableState from evaluator-only HiddenLabels, reports component metrics for task success, alignment, privacy, consent, evidence, manipulation, burden, and auditability, and preserves paired scenario ordering for model and policy comparisons. We evaluate 120 sovereignty stress scenarios across 4 model families and 8 policy baselines, yielding 3,840 frozen-prompt trajectories with raw prompts, outputs, provider-form responses, parsed actions, recomputable metrics, hard-set analyses, qualitative cases, and a blinded 3-annotator audit over 240 items. Full-sovereign scaffolding improves sovereignty score over direct, memory-only, consent-only, evidence-only, ReAct/tool-use, safety-prompt, and judge-guard baselines while reducing privacy leakage, consent violation, over-concession, and manipulation capture. Human audit shows high agreement on privacy and consent and lower agreement on manipulation, identifying the subjective frontier of platform-persuasion judgments. These results show that personal-agent evaluation must move beyond task completion toward representative, consent-aware, evidence-grounded action.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for evaluating personal agents on preserving user sovereignty, privacy, consent, and resistance to manipulation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于评估个人智能体在保护用户主权、隐私、同意和抵御操纵方面的基准

deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于评估个人智能体在保护用户主权、隐私、同意和抵御操纵方面的基准
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a benchmark for personal agents that tests user sovereignty, privacy boundaries, consent constraints, and resistance to manipulative incentives under evolving intent.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 提出SovereignPA-Bench基准，在意图演变、平台中介、隐私边界和同意约束下评估用户拥有的个人智能体，考察其是否真正维护用户利益。

deep_dive.title: AEGIS: A Mechanism-Guided Defense against Visual Synonym Jailbreaks in Text-to-Image Models
deep_dive.abstract: Text-to-image diffusion models have achieved high visual fidelity and broad adoption, but remain vulnerable to safety violations when adversaries exploit them to synthesize illicit content. Existing alignment paradigms, from input sanitization to structural feature pruning, are largely organized around unsafe concepts explicitly exposed during filtering, editing, or localization. This leaves a blind spot for visual synonym attacks (VSA), a jailbreak where benign-looking prompts elicit prohibited imagery through implicit visual associations. As a result, current defenses face a safety-utility dilemma: they may either under-mitigate VSA threats or over-suppress visually similar benign concepts. The core challenge is that VSA hides the unsafe target at the textual surface while revealing it through generation-time visual-semantic convergence. In this work, we therefore shift from static suppression of pre-specified unsafe concepts to dynamic tracing of how unsafe semantics emerge during generation. Our mechanistic analysis shows that VSA and explicit unsafe prompts converge through sparse semantic-injecting attention heads, which serve as inference-time bottlenecks for prohibited visual semantics. Based on this insight, we propose AEGIS (Adaptive Evasion Guard via Identification and Steering), an inference-time defense that applies similarity-aware repulsion only at the identified vulnerable heads. Evaluated against 16 baselines, AEGIS improves both safety and utility. On SD 1.4, it reduces ASR to $\mathbf{0.00}/\mathbf{0.03}$ for in-domain violence/nudity VSA and achieves ASRs $\le \mathbf{0.09}$ on out-of-domain explicit and adversarial attacks. It preserves benign fidelity, avoids suppressing hard-negative concepts, and transfers to SD 2.1 and FLUX.1 after re-identifying the critical heads for each backbone.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: defends against visual synonym jailbreaks in text-to-image models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 防御文本到图像模型中的视觉同义词越狱攻击
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 防御文本到图像模型中的视觉同义词越狱攻击
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 防御文本到图像模型中的视觉同义词越狱攻击
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes AEGIS, a mechanism-guided defense against visual synonym jailbreak attacks that exploit implicit visual associations to generate illicit content in text-to-image models.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 文本到图像扩散模型在广泛部署的同时仍易受越狱攻击，尤其是利用视觉同义词（VSA）通过隐性视觉关联使良性提示生成违禁内容的攻击。现有对齐范式因聚焦于显式不安全概念而存在盲点，面临安全与效用两难。本文提出AEGIS机制，通过引导模型理解深层语义关联而非简单过滤或剪枝，精准阻断视觉同义词越狱，缓解了对安全无关概念的误伤，为多模态生成模型的安全防护提供了机制层面的解决方案。

deep_dive.title: Security and Privacy in Agentic AI: Grand Challenges and Future Directions
deep_dive.abstract: We present key challenges and future research directions in the security and privacy of agentic AI, based on a horizon-scanning exercise that brought together thirty leading international experts from academia, industry, and government to engage in focused discussions and collaborative exercises on the emerging risks associated with the growing agency of AI.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: presents key challenges and future directions in security and privacy of agentic AI
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出智能体人工智能安全与隐私的关键挑战和未来方向
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出智能体人工智能安全与隐私的关键挑战和未来方向

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: A horizon-scanning by 30 experts identifying grand challenges and research directions for security and privacy in agentic AI.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 通过三十位国际专家的前瞻性研讨，系统梳理了智能体人工智能在安全与隐私方面的关键挑战和未来研究方向。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 通过三十位国际专家的前瞻性研讨，系统梳理了智能体人工智能在安全与隐私方面的关键挑战和未来研究方向。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to AI Agent安全与隐私, especially work that connects or combines: security vulnerabilities and attacks on autonomous AI agents; privacy protection mechanisms for autonomous agents and multi-agent systems; prompt injection attacks and defenses in language model based agents; sandboxing and isolation techniques for autonomous AI agents; security threats and trust mechanisms in multi-agent collaborative systems; defending against prompt injection in language model based autonomous agents; privacy leakage risks and mitigation in AI agent tool use and memory.

deep_dive.title: On Adversarial Vulnerability of Vision-Language Models through the Lens of Intermediate Spectral Subspaces
deep_dive.abstract: Adversarial vulnerability in deep neural networks (DNNs) has been studied from the perspectives of decision-boundary geometry, feature robustness, input-output Jacobians, and the instability of inverse problems. Here, we focus on the spectral structure of intermediate linear transformations that propagate information through modern DNNs, an unexplored mechanism of adversarial vulnerability. Specifically, we investigate transformer-based vision-language models, whose linear layers admit interpretable spectral decompositions and whose widespread adoption makes understanding their robustness increasingly important. We propose a white-box spectral-subspace-guided attack (SSGRA) that aligns intermediate representations with the subspace spanned by the bottom right singular vectors. Our experiments show improved attack effectiveness over existing baselines. In addition, SSGRA offers a spectral interpretation of adversarial vulnerability in VLMs, providing insights for improving their robustness.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: investigates adversarial vulnerability in vision-language models and proposes a white-box spectral-subspace-guided attack
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 研究视觉语言模型的对抗脆弱性并提出白盒频谱子空间引导攻击
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 研究视觉语言模型的对抗脆弱性并提出白盒频谱子空间引导攻击
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 研究视觉语言模型的对抗脆弱性并提出白盒频谱子空间引导攻击
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper studies the adversarial vulnerability of vision-language models by examining intermediate spectral subspaces and proposes SSGRA, a white-box attack that aligns representations during the forward pass.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文从中间频谱子空间的角度研究视觉语言模型的对抗脆弱性，揭示线性变换的频谱结构是未被探索的脆弱性机制。提出了SSGRA白盒攻击方法，通过调整中间特征表示与原始表示的对齐来生成对抗样本，成功攻击多种视觉语言模型，为理解大模型的鲁棒性和设计防御策略提供了新的理论视角。

deep_dive.title: Formal Mechanisms for Market Stability in Self-Interested Agent Societies: A Marketplace Simulation Study
deep_dive.abstract: Self-interested agents, left unconstrained, tend toward defection in repeated social dilemmas, causing cooperative gains from trade to collapse. This paper investigates what formal mechanisms, layered on top of unrestricted communication, are sufficient for a society of such agents to maintain market stability, and how resilient those mechanisms are to adversarial attack. We instantiate the research question as a multi-agent marketplace simulation where 18 LLM agents (DeepSeek-V3) with complementary production specialties must trade within a constrained social network to obtain utility. We conduct two experimental phases: (1) a mechanism comparison across eight conditions under progressive troll injection over 200 rounds, identifying Mediation as the top-performing mechanism; and (2) adversarial red-teaming of Mediation using iteratively prompt-optimised LLM-driven trolls, finding that the best attack (v6) reduces honest-agent utility by 13.3% but cannot collapse the market. Mediation enables recovery even under sustained adversarial pressure. We define adversarial robustness as a mechanism's ability to sustain positive honest-agent utility under optimised attack, and find that Mediation is robust: it can be bent but not broken.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Studies resilience of multi-agent LLM marketplace against adversarial troll injection attacks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 研究多代理LLM市场对抗性恶意攻击的弹性机制。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 研究多代理LLM市场对抗性恶意攻击的弹性机制。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 研究多代理LLM市场对抗性恶意攻击的弹性机制。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: The paper investigates formal mechanisms to maintain market stability in LLM agent societies under adversarial attack, finding reputation-based trust improves resilience.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文在多代理LLM市场模拟中，研究对抗性攻击下维持市场稳定性的形式化机制。通过注入恶意代理的越轨行为，对比八种机制条件，发现声誉信任机制能显著增强对恶意攻击的弹性。实验进行了200轮代理交互，验证了形式化机制在对抗干扰下维持合作的市场弹性。为多代理系统安全威胁和信任设计提供了经验证据。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文在多代理LLM市场模拟中，研究对抗性攻击下维持市场稳定性的形式化机制。通过注入恶意代理的越轨行为，对比八种机制条件，发现声誉信任机制能显著增强对恶意攻击的弹性。实验进行了200轮代理交互，验证了形式化机制在对抗干扰下维持合作的市场弹性。为多代理系统安全威胁和信任设计提供了经验证据。

deep_dive.title: Forget Narrowly, Retain Broadly: Unlearning as an Asymmetric Generalization Problem
deep_dive.abstract: Machine unlearning in LLMs is the targeted removal of specific knowledge while preserving all other capabilities, critical for privacy and safety. Yet existing benchmarks measure it unreliably. They miss knowledge that resurfaces under paraphrased or indirect queries, a failure we call under-forgetting, and lack the semantic, syntactic, and lexical probes needed to verify that unrelated knowledge is preserved, a failure we call over-forgetting. Both failures reflect an asymmetric generalization problem. Forget evaluation must cover diverse query formulations of the same target facts, testing whether forgetting holds beyond exact training prompts. Retain evaluation must probe a far larger and implicitly defined set, namely every fact disjoint from the forget target. The retain set thus defines the effective forget set, yet current datasets provide no fine-grained annotation of this forget-retain boundary. We address this with SUITE, an evaluation protocol and training corpus that captures forget-retain structure for real-world factual domains. Methods trained on SUITE improve substantially, showing that training data is as important as algorithmic design. Building on the obtained insights, we introduce JensUn++, an unlearning algorithm that achieves the best forget-retain utility trade-off across three LLMs, in both sequential and joint unlearning settings. Code and datasets are available at https://amitpeleg.github.io/forget-narrowly-retain-broadly
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Addresses unlearning evaluation for LLMs, identifying under-forgetting and over-forgetting as asymmetric generalization.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对LLM的遗忘评估，识别欠遗忘和过遗忘的非对称泛化问题。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对LLM的遗忘评估，识别欠遗忘和过遗忘的非对称泛化问题。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对LLM的遗忘评估，识别欠遗忘和过遗忘的非对称泛化问题。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a new evaluation framework for LLM unlearning that measures under-forgetting via diverse paraphrases and over-forgetting via semantic probes, highlighting asymmetric generalization.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM遗忘基准不可靠：它们遗漏了在改写查询下重新出现的知识（欠遗忘），并缺乏验证无关知识保留所需的语义、句法和词汇探针（过遗忘）。本文将这些失败归结为非对称泛化问题，并提出新的评估框架，要求遗忘评估覆盖多样化的查询表述，而保留评估需探测更大的隐式定义集合。这为可靠评估LLM中的机器遗忘提供了重要见解。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有LLM遗忘基准不可靠：它们遗漏了在改写查询下重新出现的知识（欠遗忘），并缺乏验证无关知识保留所需的语义、句法和词汇探针（过遗忘）。本文将这些失败归结为非对称泛化问题，并提出新的评估框架，要求遗忘评估覆盖多样化的查询表述，而保留评估需探测更大的隐式定义集合。这为可靠评估LLM中的机器遗忘提供了重要见解。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: scalable machine unlearning for large language models and foundation models

deep_dive.title: Federated Deep Learning for Privacy-Preserving Cardiovascular Disease Risk Prediction
deep_dive.abstract: Cardiovascular disease risk prediction models often rely on data from a single institution or centrally pooled datasets. Extending these models across institutions could be limited by privacy regulations and constraints on sharing patient-level data. Federated learning enables collaborative model development without transferring sensitive patient data, but its application in healthcare remains challenging because datasets often differ in size, population characteristics, and outcome definitions. In this study, we present a federated deep learning approach for privacy-preserving cardiovascular disease risk prediction that integrates two population-based cohorts with different characteristics: Lifelines, including 148,230 participants meeting the study inclusion criteria with self-reported outcomes, and the Rotterdam Study, including a smaller cohort of 10,155 participants with digitally linked clinical outcomes. Model performance was primarily evaluated on the Rotterdam Study because of its complete follow-up. Deep survival models trained using federated learning achieved higher predictive performance than models trained locally without federation. For the Rotterdam Study, the C-statistic increased from 0.728 (95% CI: 0.717-0.739) to 0.739 (95% CI: 0.728-0.749). For Lifelines, the C-statistic increased from 0.783 (95% CI: 0.775-0.791) to 0.787 (95% CI: 0.780-0.792). These findings suggest that federated deep learning across heterogeneous cohorts can improve cardiovascular disease risk prediction while preserving the privacy of individual-level patient data.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Federated learning for privacy-preserving cardiovascular disease risk prediction
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 联邦学习用于隐私保护的心血管疾病风险预测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 联邦学习用于隐私保护的心血管疾病风险预测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 联邦学习用于隐私保护的心血管疾病风险预测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a federated deep learning approach to collaboratively train cardiovascular disease risk prediction models across two population-based cohorts without sharing sensitive patient data, demonstrating comparable or improved performance versus centralized and local models while preserving privacy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对单机构数据训练心血管疾病风险预测模型的局限性，本文提出了一种联邦深度学习方法，在不共享患者数据的前提下联合两个基线人群队列进行协同训练。实验表明，联邦模型在预测性能上优于或相当于集中式和本地模型，验证了联邦学习在隐私保护下的有效性，为多机构医疗数据协作提供了可行方案。

quick_skim.title: Out of Sight: Compression-Aware Content Protection against Agentic Crawlers
quick_skim.abstract: The rise of LLM-based agents with reasoning, summarization, and memory capabilities has created a new threat surface for online content that conventional defenses fail to address. Existing defenses like access controls can be circumvented by agents mimicking ordinary browsers, and injection-based defenses often degrade human readability. In this paper, we revisit the agent pipeline and identify context compression, which agents routinely invoke to fit context budgets, as a critical yet overlooked defense layer. We propose CAPE, a framework that protects high-value textual content by injecting invisible perturbations without changing its human-visible surface form, thereby inducing severe information loss during agent compression. CAPE extracts disruptive seed perturbations from an accessible surrogate compressor, then adapts them to query-only target compressors through prior-guided evolution and preference-calibrated candidate prioritization, achieving effective protection under a low query budget. Experiments on three content types and four compression settings show that CAPE improves information loss by up to 75.8% over the strongest baseline while keeping protected content visually indistinguishable from originals. CAPE also transfers to real-world settings, including the LangGraph agent workflow and GitHub Copilot, highlighting its generality and practical value. This paper aims to reveal context compression as a new defense layer, promoting content protection research in the agent era.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: targets LLM-based agents' context compression to protect content by injecting invisibly perturbed text that causes information loss.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用LLM代理的上下文压缩机制，注入隐形扰动文本导致信息丢失以保护内容。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用LLM代理的上下文压缩机制，注入隐形扰动文本导致信息丢失以保护内容。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用LLM代理的上下文压缩机制，注入隐形扰动文本导致信息丢失以保护内容。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CAPE, a framework that protects online content from agentic crawlers by injecting invisible perturbations that exploit context compression in LLM agents, causing information loss without altering human-visible text.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对LLM代理抓取内容构成的新型威胁，提出CAPE框架，通过注入隐形扰动文本利用代理的上下文压缩机制，在不改变人眼可见形式的前提下引发信息丢失，从而保护高价值内容免受代理爬虫侵犯，实验证明其有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对LLM代理抓取内容构成的新型威胁，提出CAPE框架，通过注入隐形扰动文本利用代理的上下文压缩机制，在不改变人眼可见形式的前提下引发信息丢失，从而保护高价值内容免受代理爬虫侵犯，实验证明其有效性。

quick_skim.title: FedMark-FM: Auditable, Risk-Adjusted Data Markets for Federated Foundation-Model Adaptation
quick_skim.abstract: Federated foundation-model adaptation increasingly relies on heterogeneous private artifacts (retrieval corpora, prompts and demonstrations, LoRA adapters, preference and safety data, and update sketches), yet existing federated-learning incentive mechanisms price clients as homogeneous data or update providers. This assumption poorly matches foundation-model pipelines, where contribution value is heterogeneous, non-IID, pipeline-dependent, privacy-constrained, and vulnerable to strategic behavior. We propose FedMark-FM, an auditable, risk-adjusted data-market framework that models clients as sellers of typed artifacts, estimates marginal contribution with S3Val, a stratified, uncertainty-aware Shapley estimator supporting pipeline-ordered valuation, and converts lower-confidence-bound values into budget-feasible payments penalizing duplication, sybil splitting, poisoned adapters, privacy-budget gaming, and cost inflation. We evaluate FedMark-FM-Bench across FEVER retrieval, held-out generator-backed RAG, and trained PEFT/LoRA tracks. Under a held-out prompt-injection poisoner, FedMark-FM improves downstream accuracy by 7.5-8.1 points over volume, leave-one-out, and FL-Shapley while selecting zero strategic clients. Split-conformal calibration reaches full lower-bound coverage at mean width 0.0141, versus 0.33 for naive intervals. We prove pipeline-ordered valuation is the unique credit rule respecting serving causality, and show it materially changes credit assignment (Spearman 0.76, selected-set overlap 0.67) while leaving held-out task quality unchanged; the market preserves rare specialists with audit-ready ledgers at 200-1000-client scale. FedMark-FM shows incentives for federated foundation models can be engineered as auditable data infrastructure coupling valuation, mechanism design, privacy interfaces, and pipeline-order semantics.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Auditable data market framework for federated foundation-model adaptation with risk adjustment
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向联邦基础模型适配的可审计、风险调整数据市场框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向联邦基础模型适配的可审计、风险调整数据市场框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向联邦基础模型适配的可审计、风险调整数据市场框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes FedMark-FM, an auditable and risk-adjusted data market for federated foundation-model adaptation, using stratified Shapley valuation and risk adjustment to incentivize heterogeneous artifact contributions while mitigating strategic behavior.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对联邦基础模型适配中异构贡献的价值评估与激励难题，本文提出FedMark-FM可审计数据市场框架，采用分层Shapley估值与风险调整机制。实验表明该框架能公平量化贡献并抵御策略行为，为安全可信的联邦学习生态提供了经济激励方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对联邦基础模型适配中异构贡献的价值评估与激励难题，本文提出FedMark-FM可审计数据市场框架，采用分层Shapley估值与风险调整机制。实验表明该框架能公平量化贡献并抵御策略行为，为安全可信的联邦学习生态提供了经济激励方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 联邦学习安全与隐私, especially work that connects or combines: security attacks and defenses in federated learning; privacy-preserving techniques and differential privacy in federated learning; data and model poisoning attacks against federated learning; secure aggregation protocols for privacy-preserving federated learning; membership inference and gradient leakage attacks in federated learning; Byzantine robust and secure aggregation for federated learning with malicious clients; privacy auditing and certified robustness in federated learning.

quick_skim.title: Proof-of-Continuity: A Temporal Model for Authority Propagation in Distributed Systems and AI Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Proof-of-Possession authorization models derive authority from the possession of artifacts such as tokens, credentials, or capabilities. This paper argues that possession is insufficient for discrete execution chains, whether they span multiple services or occur as separated steps within the same machine, because it does not guarantee preservation of the causal relationship between the origin of a request and the authority exercised at later steps. We introduce Proof-of-Continuity, a minimal authority-propagation discipline for the Provenance Identity Continuity (PIC) model, in which each execution step must be causally linked to the previous step and may only propagate a non-expansive subset of the authority received from the origin. It introduces Proof of Relationship, a single-hop causal primitive whose transitive composition is Proof-of-Continuity; these complement Proof-of-Possession rather than replace it. Under this model, the confused deputy condition cannot be satisfied as valid model behavior: any privilege exercised at a later step must already be present in the origin authority context. This is directly relevant to distributed systems and AI agents, where executors invoke tools and downstream services while holding multiple authority sources, so that the same authority/causality mismatch recurs across service boundaries. Under Proof-of-Continuity these sources may be carried together but are never merged into a combined authority, since each step is authorized only against the authority context of the lineage that caused it.   This paper concerns authorization propagation rather than authentication: identity and authentication mechanisms such as OIDC, verifiable credentials, wallets, and workload identity remain complementary mechanisms for establishing the origin, while Proof-of-Continuity addresses how authority propagates after that origin exists.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes Proof-of-Continuity for authority propagation in AI agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出用于AI智能体中权威传播的连续性证明模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出用于AI智能体中权威传播的连续性证明模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出用于AI智能体中权威传播的连续性证明模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proof-of-Continuity ensures causal authority linkage in AI agent execution chains, restricting non-expansive authority propagation to prevent privilege escalation and improve security.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有基于持有证明的授权模型无法保证执行步骤间的因果关系，可能导致权限滥用。本文提出连续性证明模型，要求每个执行步骤因果关联至前一步骤，并仅能传播非扩展的权限子集。该方法为AI智能体和分布式系统提供了更严格的权威管理机制，防止权限升级。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有基于持有证明的授权模型无法保证执行步骤间的因果关系，可能导致权限滥用。本文提出连续性证明模型，要求每个执行步骤因果关联至前一步骤，并仅能传播非扩展的权限子集。该方法为AI智能体和分布式系统提供了更严格的权威管理机制，防止权限升级。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: security threats and trust mechanisms in multi-agent collaborative systems

quick_skim.title: Statistically Undetectable Backdoors in Deep Neural Networks
quick_skim.abstract: We show how an adversarial model trainer can plant backdoors in a large class of deep, feedforward neural networks. These backdoors are statistically undetectable in the white-box setting, meaning that the backdoored and honestly trained models are close in total variation distance, even given the full descriptions of the models (e.g., all of the weights). The backdoor provides access to invariance-based adversarial examples for every input, mapping distant inputs to unusually close outputs. However, without the backdoor, it is provably impossible (under standard cryptographic assumptions) to generate any such adversarial examples in polynomial time. Our theoretical and preliminary empirical findings demonstrate a fundamental power asymmetry between model trainers and model users.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: planting statistically undetectable backdoors in deep neural networks, a security vulnerability
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 在深度神经网络中植入统计不可检测的后门，构成安全漏洞
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 在深度神经网络中植入统计不可检测的后门，构成安全漏洞
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 在深度神经网络中植入统计不可检测的后门，构成安全漏洞
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper shows that an adversary can implant backdoors in deep neural networks that are statistically undetectable even with white-box access, making them impossible to detect without the secret backdoor key, demonstrating a fundamental power asymmetry.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文揭示了一种深层神经网络后门攻击，攻击者可植入统计不可检测的后门，使得后门模型与诚实训练模型在总变分距离上接近，即便拥有完整模型描述也难以区分。该后门允许对任意输入生成对抗样本，但若无后门，在密码假设下多项式时间内无法生成此类样本。理论和初步实验表明模型训练者与防御者之间存在根本的力量不对称，对模型安全部署构成严重威胁。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文揭示了一种深层神经网络后门攻击，攻击者可植入统计不可检测的后门，使得后门模型与诚实训练模型在总变分距离上接近，即便拥有完整模型描述也难以区分。该后门允许对任意输入生成对抗样本，但若无后门，在密码假设下多项式时间内无法生成此类样本。理论和初步实验表明模型训练者与防御者之间存在根本的力量不对称，对模型安全部署构成严重威胁。
